# Current Sources

Use primary sources when making current claims about AI search, crawlers, and publisher controls.

## Sources

- llms.txt proposal: https://llmstxt.org/
- Google AI features and websites: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/ai-overviews
- Google Search essentials: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/essentials
- Google robots.txt docs: https://developers.google.com/search/reference/robots_txt
- OpenAI crawlers and user agents: https://platform.openai.com/docs/bots
- Perplexity crawlers: https://docs.perplexity.ai/docs/resources/perplexity-crawlers
- Perplexity robots.txt help: https://www.perplexity.ai/help-center/
- Schema.org validator: https://validator.schema.org/
- Schema.org TechArticle: https://schema.org/TechArticle
- Schema.org FAQPage: https://schema.org/FAQPage

## Ground Rules

- Do not claim that `llms.txt` guarantees indexing, ranking, or citation.
- Do not promise AI assistant citations.
- Separate training crawlers, search crawlers, and user-triggered fetchers.
- Prefer canonical public pages over special bot-only content.
- Use current official docs for bot tokens, robots behavior, and publisher controls.
